prompt: A Community Health Enrollment & Clinic Commerce system with main features including offline enrollment and ordering, a pricing and promotion engine, health-check report management, in-app notifications, cultural content publishing, and risk control. The React-based user experience serves three primary roles: Patients/Members, Clinic Staff, and Administrators, with a separate Reviewer role for clinical sign-off. Patients can browse locally published cultural and wellness content (articles, image galleries, audio/video) in a clean, accessible interface, save drafts of enrollment applications, and submit an enrollment package that may include add-on services (for example, “Annual Lab Panel” or “Nutrition Session”) to create an order. At checkout, the UI shows a transparent “best offer applied” breakdown with line-level discount reasoning (e.g., 10% off over $200.00, buy-one-get-one on select screenings, or 50% off the second item), and prevents stacking when rules are mutually exclusive. Clinic Staff can capture structured health-check results during a visit, immediately see reference ranges and abnormal flags, compare against prior results by date, and assemble reports from templates; the patient-facing view clearly indicates “Reviewed” status and provides one-click PDF export for printing or local sharing. Administrators manage promotion rules with effective windows, publish/withdraw cultural content through a draft–review–publish workflow with sensitive-word warnings, and monitor an in-app notification inbox that highlights upcoming due dates, overdue balances, and “hold available” pickups, while enforcing frequency limits such as no more than three reminders per item per 24 hours.The backend uses NestJS to implement a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the React client over the local network or on the same machine, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for users, enrollments, seat quotas, orders, promotion rules, reports, content versions, notifications, and risk events. The order-to-payment loop is implemented as an offline “recorded payment” workflow: Staff may register payment method types like cash, check, or manual card terminal, capture a reference number, and mark orders as Paid, Refunded, or Canceled; unpaid orders auto-cancel after 30 minutes of inactivity, and refunds require a reason code and supervisor confirmation. The promotion engine evaluates rules by priority, time window, and mutual exclusion, computes the best discount deterministically, and persists an immutable discount audit trail per order for traceability and dispute handling. Health-check results support version management: edits create a new version, require a Reviewer e-signature (username/password re-entry) within 24 hours, and lock previous signed versions from modification. Risk control enforces local-only protections: username/password login with 12+ characters and lockout after five failed attempts for 15 minutes, device fingerprinting based on stable local attributes, IP-based allow/deny lists, per-action rate limits (e.g., 30 requests/minute per user), and CAPTCHA for suspicious spikes using locally generated challenges. Anomaly rules detect promo abuse, bulk registrations, and repeated refund attempts, generating risk alerts and incident tickets with hit logs for administrator review. All sensitive fields (such as medical identifiers and notes) are encrypted at rest, PDFs are stored locally with checksum validation, and the system runs fully offline with no dependencies on external identity, messaging, payment, maps, cloud storage, or third-party APIs.
